get.description: Every Agent including non-public ones, with visibility configuration and the managed (v2) configuration block. Admin role required.

post.description: Create an Agent with admin-only fields (is_public, estimated_manual_minutes). Admin role required.

put.description: Merge-update any Agent's configuration. Admin role required.

delete.description: Hard-delete an Agent. Admin role required.

put.description: Update the estimated manual minutes used for time-saved statistics. Admin role required.
Restrict a non-public Agent to a list of user ids. Admin role required.

get.description: Whether the calling user has the admin role.
Paginated Platform Feedback inbox. Admin role required.
One-shot health probes: OpenSandbox API + sandbox count, Redis, Asynq queue depths, OSS, and an Active Run summary. A failing probe darkens only its own card. Admin role required.

Bidirectional cross-check: Active Runs whose sandbox vanished (orphan records, candidates for finalization) and sandboxes no run references (orphan sandboxes, candidates for deletion). Admin role required.
The unified Active Run inventory (v1 Tasks + v2 Sessions, non-terminal), annotated with staleness and ordered by suspicion (longest-silent first). Admin role required.

On-demand read-only cross-check of one run (kind task|session): joins the control-plane record, the Sandbox Service view and workspace evidence tails into a stall shape classification. Admin role required.

post.description: Admin intervention: finalize a record whose sandbox is already gone (orphaned record). Admin role required.
Admin intervention: destroy the run's sandbox and finalize the record as terminated. Admin role required.
Admin intervention: gracefully interrupt one active run. Recorded as a session.user_action Thread Event with the acting admin's identity. Admin role required.

delete.description: Admin intervention: delete a sandbox no Active Run references. Admin role required.

get.description: Bounded tail read of one workspace file (max 256 KiB; truncation flagged). Read-only. Admin role required.
List a live sandbox workspace directory (default /tmp/workspace, glob pattern default *). Read-only. Admin role required.
Platform-wide settings: default harness images and the Omni Bar interpreter model config. Admin role required.

put.description: Partial update; omitted fields are kept. Admin role required.

get.description: Aggregated platform stats over the last N days (default 30), merging v1 Tasks and v2 Sessions. Admin role required.
All platform users with task counts. Admin role required.

put.description: Grant or revoke the admin role. Admin role required.

get.description: List Agents visible to the caller: public Agents for anonymous users, plus per-user authorized Agents when a Bearer token is supplied (auth optional).

post.description: Upsert an Agent with merge-update semantics: omitted fields keep stored values; non-nil arrays/objects replace the whole set. v1 image agents require image+entrypoint; presence of any v2 managed field (engine, instructions, skills, ...) marks the Agent as managed and mints a new Agent Version on effective change. No auth required.
Toggle the archived flag. Archiving blocks new Sessions and new Subagent references while history stays readable. No auth required (same trust group as register).
Authenticate with an AiHub account and obtain a Bearer token for all authenticated endpoints. No auth required.
Store a write-only secret. The plaintext value is never returned by any API; it only exists briefly in Worker memory and is injected into sandbox MCP config files (0600), never into sandbox env vars.

get.description: Non-secret view of the caller's saved credentials (never includes plaintext values).

delete.description: Delete one of the caller's credentials.

post.description: Bulk-add one task to several datasets at once.
post.summary: Add a task to multiple datasets

get.description: Datasets owned by or visible to the caller.
Dataset metadata plus a paginated page of items (limit/offset).

post.description: Enqueue an async export job; poll GET /dataset/export/:exportId for progress and the download URL.
Add one historical task/session (by task_id) to the dataset.

put.description: Restrict the dataset to a list of user ids.

get.description: Human-readable, interactive rendering of this document (embedded Swagger UI). Use the Authorize button with 'Bearer <token>' to try authenticated endpoints. Public, no authentication required. Response is text/html, not JSON; static assets are served under /api/docs/<file>.

post.description: Store free-form Platform Feedback with the page it was written on.
Upload one file to OSS as multipart/form-data with field name 'file' (max 100 MB). The returned oss_key is used in task submissions and session input_files.

Register one immutable MCP Registry entry version. transport http: url + auth_header/auth_scheme + static headers (contributes one egress whitelist host per Session). transport stdio: command + args + env; the bound credential is injected via credential_env (Sessions selecting any stdio server run without egress restriction).

get.description: Default: unarchived public view across all owners. scope=mine: only the caller's own entries, including archived ones.

delete.description: Hard-delete one of the caller's own entry versions. Allowed only when no Agent configuration references it.

post.description: Toggle archived on one of the caller's own entries. Existing Agent references keep resolving.
post.summary: Archive or restore an MCP entry

get.description: The caller's own managed Agents with full configuration (suitable for editing).

post.description: Self-service creation of a v2 managed Agent owned by the caller. Accepts the same managed fields as /agent/register (engine, instructions, start_prompt_template, skills, mcp_bindings, credential_slots, subagents, ...).

put.description: Merge-update one of the caller's managed Agents: omitted fields keep stored values; non-nil arrays/objects replace the whole set. Effective configuration changes mint a new immutable Agent Version.

post.description: Toggle archived on one of the caller's managed Agents. Archiving blocks new Sessions and new Subagent references; history stays readable.
Record what the user did with a Command Draft: confirmed, edited or dismissed.

One stateless interpretation of free-form user text into a typed Command Draft: kind is create_agent, start_session, create_skill, feedback or cannot_interpret. Makes a synchronous model call (its own 120s timeout budget). Report what the user did with the draft via POST /omni/interactions/:interactionId/outcome.

get.description: Returns this OpenAPI (Swagger 2.0) JSON document describing every Gateway endpoint. Public, no authentication required. Intended for both human tooling and AI agents. A human-readable Swagger UI rendering is served at GET /api/docs.

post.description: Create and enqueue a v2 Session against a managed Agent (engine non-empty, not archived). Validates Agent, version and credential bindings before enqueueing; unresolvable required credential slots fail immediately. Follow progress via GET /session/:sessionId/events.

get.description: List the caller's Sessions, newest first. limit defaults to 20 (max 100), offset to 0.

Full Session detail: status + stop/termination reason, resources, credential audit (ids only, never plaintext), MCP servers (non-secret view), skills, subagents, threads, deliverables, evidence archive, and all persisted Thread Events with event_cursor. Owner or public; auth optional.

post.description: Explicitly end a retained idle Session (PRD 020): finalization runs asynchronously — final deliverable collection, evidence archive, sandbox deletion. After this the Session is no longer resumable; Rerun remains available. Idempotent. Owner only.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream: an init snapshot (full SessionResp including event history and threads), then live Thread Events by type, then done when the Session reaches idle/terminated; timeout after 30 min. Owner or public; pass ?token=<bearer token>. Response is text/event-stream, not JSON.

post.description: Send a Follow-up Message (PRD 020) into a resumable idle Session: the Session transitions back to running and the message is delivered to the retained sandbox as a new Session Turn with full prior context. 409 when the Session is running, not resumable, or already finalized. Owner only.
Request an asynchronous User Interrupt of a running Session. The Worker stops the engine and the Session ends as idle with stop_reason=user_interrupted. Owner only.

get.description: Editable starting data of a source Session (agent, model, start prompt values, initial message, input file refs) used to prefill a new-Session form. Rerun always creates a new Session; it never resumes the source sandbox.

post.description: Register one immutable Skill version as multipart/form-data. Fields: slug, version, name, description, plus either an 'artifact' file (zip/tgz containing a top-level SKILL.md, normalized to a canonical artifact) or 'skill_md' text (editor mode, optionally with repeated 'files' parts and comma-separated 'exec_paths'). version must not be the literal 'latest'; duplicate slug@version is rejected.

get.description: Default: unarchived public view across all owners. scope=mine: only the caller's own entries, including archived ones.
Skill Workbench detail for one owner/slug: every version visible to the viewer, newest first, each with its live-config reference count.

post.description: Publish the next immutable version of owner/slug from a base version plus a changeset (ADR 0022). Publishing is open (PRD 024): any signed-in caller may publish into another user's slug as a Skill Collaborator; the slug owner stays fixed and the caller is recorded as the version's publisher. multipart/form-data: base_version, version (new), optional name/description, repeated file parts with artifact-relative paths, and repeated 'delete' values for removed paths.
post.summary: Publish a new skill version (owner or collaborator)

get.description: The whole artifact of one skill version transcoded to a zip archive (application/zip attachment).
Raw bytes of one file inside the artifact (?path=SKILL.md). Content-Type is sniffed from the content; response is the file body, not JSON.
File tree of one skill version's artifact, with text/binary hints.
Which live Agent configurations reference this skill version: platform-wide total plus the referencing Agents the viewer is allowed to see.

delete.description: Hard-delete one of the caller's own skill versions. Allowed only when no Agent configuration references it.

post.description: Toggle archived on one of the caller's own skill versions. Archived versions disappear from pickers but existing Agent references keep resolving.
post.summary: Archive or restore a skill version

post.description: Internal Worker -> Gateway callback that records a task's terminal status, output files and Claude messages. Not intended for external callers.

get.description: List the caller's tasks, newest first. limit defaults to 20 (max 100), offset to 0. scope=all lists all tasks visible to the caller. search filters by case-insensitive substring across id, agent, model, status, and (scope=all) owner username.

post.description: Queue a run of an Agent. For v1 image agents this creates a Task; if agent_id points to a v2 managed Agent this transparently creates a Session instead (task_id == session_id, params map to start prompt values, input_files become Session File Resources).
Cancel a queued or running task; destroys its sandbox if one was created. Owner only.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of live task updates. Event types: init, status, progress, claude_log, completed, failed, cancelled, done, timeout (30 min). Owner or public; pass ?token=<bearer token> since EventSource cannot set headers. Response is text/event-stream, not JSON.
All user feedback entries recorded on the task.

put.description: Rate a finished task: rating 1 (up) or -1 (down) plus an optional comment. One feedback row per user per task (upsert).

get.description: All metric feedback entries recorded on the task.

post.description: Score one metric declared in the Agent's metrics (PRD 003). Numeric metrics use score; categorical/boolean/text metrics use value.

put.description: Make a task publicly readable (or private again). Owner only.

get.description: Same shape as /status, oriented to the result page (includes archive info). Owner or public; auth optional.
Task detail. Accessible to the owner or anyone if the task is public; auth optional. For v2 managed Agents this facade maps Session states onto task states.

apiKey.description: Bearer token from POST /api/auth/login. Format: 'Bearer <token>'. SSE endpoints alternatively accept '?token=<token>' as a query parameter.
apiKey.name: Authorization

x-description: This is a goctl generated swagger file.
